mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising consumer credit balances indicate synchronized expansion in durable goods demand and household leverage, which historically precedes broad equity outperformance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55 percent of historical occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index above 105 reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which expands corporate profit margins and lifts S&P 500 valuations over a one-month forward window.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO readings above 105, or the signal generates negative returns in over 50 percent of triggered periods across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month acceleration in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points triggers risk-off repricing as borrowing costs rise faster than corporate earnings can adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances following a 0.25pp rate hike acceleration, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and rising consumer price inflation signals overheating demand that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and CPIAUCSL acceleration fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 55 percent of historical windows, or the combined signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP exceeding 2.5 percent confirms underlying economic momentum that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in quarters with GDP growth above 2.5 percent are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate reading below 6.0 percent combined with steady payroll additions indicates a self-reinforcing labor market recovery that boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations over a weekly forward period.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE falls below 6.0 percent, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
